summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a wide range of agenda items including approval of bills, donations for Fire Prevention Week and National First Responders Day, grant acceptances for traffic enforcement and paramedic training, and a comprehensive 2026 budget presentation by Administrator Rose. The budget presentation detailed revenue challenges across all departments, road paving funding shortfalls, restricted vs. unrestricted funds, levy structures, and capital equipment needs, with trustees providing feedback on deferring certain purchases like a dump truck, ALS vehicle, and grizzly struts while prioritizing safety-related equipment.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Sept 23 - Oct 28, 2025) passed
Motion to approve Goshen Chamber of Commerce Light Up Goshen Parade on Nov 22, 2025 passed
Motion to approve National Opioid Settlement with multiple companies (CL-1765935) passed
Motion to sell obsolete floor tiles and portable radios on GovDeals passed
Motion to approve staff attendance at OTA 2026 Annual Winter Conference (Feb 4-6, 2026) passed
Motion to approve ADP payroll provider renewal with 20% cost concession for three years passed
Motion to approve Ohio Plan insurance renewal with Highline (effective 10-23-25) with $494 premium decrease passed
Resolution R-024-2025 accepting tax amounts/rates and certifying to county auditor passed
Resolution R-025-2025 requesting speed study on Linton Road passed
Motion to accept $750 donation from CPAA for Fire Prevention Week passed
Motion to accept $751 donation from Lions Club for Fire Prevention Week passed
Motion to accept $250 in Kroger gift cards from CPAA for National First Responders Day passed
Motion to accept $250 in Chick-fil-A gift cards from CPAA for National First Responders Day passed
Motion to authorize purchase of 10-foot Norway spruce and run electric to it passed
Motion to accept $58,728.24 grant from Ohio Dept of Public Safety for overtime traffic enforcement passed
Motion to accept $28,571.42 AFG grant for paramedic training passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to meet individually with trustees to finalize 2026 budget decisions

Chief Myers to explore community donation opportunities for grizzly struts purchase
Chad Meadows to order 10-foot Norway spruce and coordinate electric installation before Christmas
Department heads to refine budget proposals based on trustee feedback on deferred purchases (dump truck, grizzly struts, second cruiser)
Budget discussion to continue at next meeting

notable_topics: 2026 comprehensive budget presentation across all departments
Road paving funding crisis - township lacks dedicated road levy unlike neighboring townships
Potential second $5 PMVL registration fee to generate ~$100,000 for roads
Community host fund (cannabis dispensary revenue) still not received statewide
Station 18 fire station construction progress - asphalt down, permanent power and gas connected, on budget
Ambulance involved in crash reducing fleet to three medic units
Speed study requested for Linton Road (40 mph zone)
Capital equipment needs discussion - cruisers, dump truck, front loader, tractor, quint, cardiac monitors
National Opioid Settlement opt-in for additional funds
Interest earnings from investing unencumbered funds significantly increased
Property tax re-evaluation impacts and potential state rollback
Fire department EMS billing lower than expected ($710K vs $825K estimate) due to Medicaid delays
John Becker announced candidacy for Clermont County Auditor
Sam Lever baseball history presentation by Jim Poe
Upcoming community events: Light Up Goshen Parade, Lions Holly Fair, Trunk or Treat
attendees: John Becker (former State Representative, county auditor candidate)
notes: Transcript was truncated before meeting conclusion. The 2026 budget was presented for discussion only with no vote taken; trustees will review individually and revisit at next meeting. Trustees expressed concern about unknown revenue streams (cannabis host funds, insurance settlement, potential property tax rollbacks) and suggested deferring non-critical capital purchases. Administrator Rose presented an unusually detailed educational budget presentation aimed at public transparency. Fire Chief Myers is getting married the following Saturday. The township has 47.1 miles of roads with only ~$87,000 in sustainable annual paving budget. Station 18 rebuild is fully funded with no cost to residents through insurance, EMA, grants, and state capital funds.
title: BOT 10-28-25
